text.title: Mandatory Rules and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete rules for ASP.NET Core async hygiene, security, and common LLM mistakes in this layered pattern.

list.item: AsNoTracking() on all read queries — doubles read throughput on Postgres/SQL Server.
Use ProblemDetails (RFC 7807) for error responses; configure AddProblemDetails() in Program.cs.
Source-generated JSON serialization ([JsonSerializable]) for hot endpoints.
Health checks for every external dependency: AddHealthChecks().AddNpgSql(...).AddRedis(...).
Use Required keyword on DTOs (.NET 7+) instead of [Required] — surfaces missing fields at compile time.

antipattern.description: async void used for "fire-and-forget" notification callbacks.
antipattern.reason: One unhandled exception inside async void crashes the host. Use Task-returning methods and background services instead.
In .NET Core 6+ this rarely deadlocks but causes thread-pool starvation. Force agents to await all the way through.

antipattern.description: Repository methods return IQueryable<T> for caller composition.
antipattern.reason: By the time the caller materializes the query, the DbContext is disposed. Always ToListAsync / ToArrayAsync inside the repo.

antipattern.description: PagedResult<T> re-invented per project (TotalCount vs Total, PageIndex vs Page).
antipattern.reason: Agents ship incompatible pagination shapes. Centralize in Common/Models and reference from every handler.
Socket exhaustion. Always inject IHttpClientFactory; Roslynator rule detects the pattern.

antipattern.description: Connection strings or JWT secrets committed in appsettings.json.
antipattern.reason: Use dotnet user-secrets in dev and env vars + Key Vault in prod. Add a pre-commit secret scanner.
